title: How integrated data is helping New Zealanders | Stats NZ
content:
## How integrated data is helping New Zealanders
Researchers use the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) to gain insights into our society and economy. These insights can help answer questions about complex issues that affect New Zealanders.
Our ‘Better data, better lives’ videos show how our partner organisations are using insights from the IDI to help New Zealanders.

## Improving the wellbeing of young people at risk
Stats NZ and Oranga Tamariki have worked together to gain insights from the IDI that will help improve the wellbeing of children and young people.
Read the transcript of the video (PDF, 248 KB)

## Improving outcomes for young people not in school or work
Methodist Mission Southern (MMS) worked with Stats NZ to better understand young people who are not in employment, education, or training (NEET) using the IDI. Results will help MMS to better target their services to reach the young people who need them.
Read the transcript of this video

## Long-term outcomes for premature babies
Researchers from the University of Otago are using the IDI to study the long-term outcomes for very premature babies. This research will help parents and doctors make better-informed decisions about how to care for these babies.

## How researchers are using the IDI
Research using Stats NZ microdata has information about all research projects that have used the IDI.
Page updated 25 July 2018
